$comment: PROTO-EARTH at the giant impact (~4.51 Ga) - a DIFFERENT BODY from modern Earth, which is the RESULT: proto-Earth + Theia - the Moon - what escaped. Pointing the birth scene at earth.json gave a post-impact planet of 1.115 Earth masses, 11% too heavy, because it added a Mars-sized body to a planet that already contained it. Mass here is 0.910 M_earth: what the budget demands, and independently what the giant-impact literature gives (~0.9). NO CONTINENTS, and no surface map, because there were none. At 4.51 Ga Earth was a MAGMA OCEAN: the oldest continental material we have is ~4.4 Ga zircon grains, the oldest surviving crust ~4.0 Ga, and paleogeographic reconstructions do not meaningfully reach past ~1 Ga. There is nothing to reconstruct, so nothing is drawn. The surface is molten peridotite rather than a basalt crust, and the declared geotherm (1,900 K at the surface) sits above peridotite's 1,393 K melt point - so MOLTEN emerges from the phase model and the glow emerges from the incandescence law. Neither is painted on. Layer radii are modern Earth's scaled by (0.910)^(1/3) at unchanged densities - FLAGGED: a less massive, hotter, fully molten planet is less compressed than this, so the real density profile differs somewhat. No atmosphere: modern air is later outgassing, and this impact would strip it regardless.
name: Proto-Earth
